payload.source_text: The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Source elements present in the ledger.

payload.evaluated_scope: All input material in this project

payload.description: Task claiming process enabling children to select available work
payload.justification: SG915 describes claiming as a core process children perform to acquire tasks

payload.description: Task completion tracking process monitoring work progress
payload.justification: SG915 identifies tracking completion as a fundamental process the enterprise must perform

payload.description: Task definition process establishing work opportunities with rewards
payload.justification: SG916 describes parents defining tasks with rewards as a strategic process

payload.description: Oversight reporting process providing parents visibility into child compensation status
payload.justification: SG919 identifies oversight reporting as a process needed to fulfill parental responsibility

payload.description: Historical accountability analysis process enabling trend examination
payload.justification: SG920 describes historical accountability and trend analysis as a required capability/process

payload.description: Pocket money task representing work assignable to children
payload.justification: SG915 and SG916 both refer to tasks as the central entity that children claim/complete and parents define with rewards

payload.description: Monetary reward associated with task completion
payload.justification: SG916 explicitly describes monetary rewards as distinct things defined by parents and associated with tasks

payload.description: Child's accomplishment record tracking completed work
payload.justification: SG917 refers to accomplishments as things of strategic importance that must be visible to children

payload.description: Weekly earnings representing child's financial achievement
payload.justification: SG918 identifies weekly earnings as a distinct entity that children need to understand and track

payload.description: Association between child and assigned tasks
payload.justification: SG915 implies a relationship where children claim tasks, establishing ownership/assignment

payload.description: Compensation commitment from parent to child
payload.justification: SG919 explicitly describes a commitment relationship between parent and child regarding compensation

payload.description: Weekly earnings cycle establishing compensation period
payload.justification: SG918 references weekly earnings, indicating a weekly cycle of strategic importance to the enterprise

payload.description: Task completion event triggering reward eligibility
payload.justification: SG915 and SG916 both reference completion as a significant event that determines reward payment

payload.description: Child user participating in pocket money economy
payload.justification: SG915, SG917, and SG918 all identify children as primary actors claiming tasks, viewing opportunities, and tracking earnings

payload.description: Parent user managing pocket money system
payload.justification: SG916 and SG919 identify parents as actors defining tasks and requiring oversight capabilities

payload.description: Enable children to access and complete earning opportunities
payload.justification: SG915 expresses the core purpose of enabling children to claim and track pocket money tasks

payload.description: Provide children visibility into available work and their achievements
payload.justification: SG917 articulates visibility into opportunities and accomplishments as a strategic purpose

payload.description: Enable children to understand their financial progress
payload.justification: SG918 captures the objective of enabling children to understand earnings achievement

payload.description: Enable parents to fulfill compensation commitments to children
payload.justification: SG919 defines the parental need to fulfill compensation commitment as a driving goal

payload.description: Provide stakeholders historical accountability capability
payload.justification: SG920 indicates historical accountability as a strategic requirement driving system design

payload.description: Enable trend analysis to inform pocket money system decisions
payload.justification: SG920 identifies trend analysis capability as a distinct strategic objective

payload.description: Parent reporting function enabling view of child performance and earnings data
payload.justification: Conceptual reporting capability for parents is described as a business function supporting payment reconciliation.

payload.description: Task claiming process whereby children select available tasks
payload.justification: Children claiming tasks is described as a core business process.

payload.description: Pocket money earning process whereby children accumulate funds through task completion
payload.justification: Earning pocket money throughout the week is described as a core business process.

payload.description: Task definition process whereby parents create tasks and assign monetary values
payload.justification: Parent capability to define tasks and values represents a business process.

payload.description: Child reporting function enabling view of aggregated weekly earnings
payload.justification: Conceptual reporting capability for children is described as a business function.

payload.description: Information visibility function providing children access to task availability and completion status
payload.justification: Conceptual information visibility requirement represents a business function defining data access.

payload.description: Completed tasks must be attributed to the specific child who performed them
payload.justification: Business rule establishing the conceptual relationship and attribution requirement.

payload.description: Child as a business entity participating in the pocket money system
payload.justification: Multiple signals reference children as actors who claim tasks, complete them, and view data—establishing Child as a core business entity.

payload.description: Parent as a business entity with authority over the pocket money system
payload.justification: Parents define tasks, set values, and view reporting data—establishing Parent as a distinct business entity with administrative capabilities.

payload.description: Task as a business entity representing work to be performed
payload.justification: Tasks are defined, claimed, completed, and reset—central business entity with lifecycle and attributes.

payload.description: Monetary value associated with each task
payload.justification: Parents define monetary values for tasks, establishing this as a core task attribute.

payload.description: Task completion status tracking whether a task has been performed
payload.justification: Children view completion status and history must be preserved, indicating this as a tracked task attribute.

payload.description: Task availability status indicating whether a task can be claimed
payload.justification: Children view task availability and it resets weekly, establishing this as a temporal task attribute.

payload.description: Aggregated weekly earnings total for each child
payload.justification: Children view aggregated weekly earnings, establishing this as a derived attribute of child performance.

payload.description: Assignment relationship between completed task and the child who performed it
payload.justification: Explicit business rule establishing the conceptual relationship between completed tasks and child entities.

payload.description: Definition relationship between parent and tasks with their monetary values
payload.justification: Parents define tasks and values, establishing a creation/ownership relationship.

payload.description: Weekly period for task availability and earnings accumulation
payload.justification: Multiple signals reference weekly period as the temporal cycle for earning, reporting, and task reset.

payload.description: Period end event triggering availability of aggregated earnings report
payload.justification: Children view aggregated weekly earnings at period end, establishing a temporal event boundary.

payload.description: Weekly boundary trigger initiating task availability reset
payload.justification: Business rule defines weekly boundaries as the trigger point for task availability reset.

payload.description: Child role with capability to claim tasks, complete them, and view earnings
payload.justification: Child is described as a business role with specific capabilities in the system.

payload.description: Parent role with authority to define tasks, set values, and view performance reports
payload.justification: Parent is described as a business role with administrative authority and reporting access.

payload.description: Enable payment reconciliation for parents through performance and earnings visibility
payload.justification: Parent reporting capability explicitly supports payment reconciliation as a business objective.

payload.description: Task completion history must be preserved for accountability and audit purposes
payload.justification: Data retention requirement mandating preservation of history reflects a business principle of accountability.

payload.description: Task availability must reset at weekly cycle boundaries
payload.justification: Business rule defining temporal reset behavior represents a constraint on task availability.

payload.description: Task completion history must be retained beyond the current reporting period
payload.justification: Data retention requirement represents a constraint on system data management behavior.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL CellContentItem elements present in the ledger.

payload.name: Child Economic Participation
payload.description: Enterprise commitment to establishing and governing the means by which children participate in the household economy through work opportunities, achievement recognition, and financial progression.

payload.name: Parental Compensation Accountability
payload.description: Enterprise obligation to define, commit to, and fulfill monetary compensation to children based on completed work, including visibility and oversight mechanisms for responsible stewardship.

payload.name: Performance Transparency Governance
payload.description: Enterprise responsibility to maintain verifiable records of work completion and compensation status, enabling stakeholder visibility, historical analysis, and informed governance decisions.

payload.name: Task Stewardship
payload.description: Business responsibility for defining, maintaining, and governing the work obligations available within the pocket money system, including their associated value propositions and lifecycle boundaries.

payload.name: Participation Accountability
payload.description: Business responsibility for establishing and maintaining the record of which participants performed which obligations, ensuring attribution integrity and preserving evidence of contribution for reconciliation purposes.

payload.name: Earnings Settlement
payload.description: Business responsibility for determining, aggregating, and reporting the financial obligations owed to participants based on their completed contributions, aligned to defined settlement periods.

payload.name: Performance Oversight
payload.description: Business responsibility for providing authoritative visibility into participant contributions and outcomes to enable informed governance decisions and payment reconciliation by those with oversight authority.

payload.name: Participant Entitlement
payload.description: Business responsibility for enabling participants to access current opportunity information and their earned entitlements, supporting informed decision-making about engagement and contribution.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Domain elements present in the ledger.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to claim available tasks.
payload.rationale: The task claiming process is the primary mechanism through which children select work opportunities; this obligation synthesizes the How-column process description with the Why-column goal of enabling access to earning opportunities.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to complete tasks.
payload.rationale: Completing tasks is the second phase of the earning opportunity lifecycle, distinct from claiming; the enterprise must provide the means for children to mark work as finished.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide children visibility into available tasks.
payload.rationale: Children must be able to discover what work is available to them before they can claim it; this is the first step in the participation flow.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide children visibility into their accomplishments.
payload.rationale: Children need to view their completed work history as a record of achievement and as the basis for understanding earnings; this obligation is distinct from viewing available work.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to understand their financial progress.
payload.rationale: Financial progress visibility is distinct from viewing accomplishments; it synthesizes earnings data into an understanding of cumulative or trend-based financial position.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain the association between children and tasks.
payload.rationale: The enterprise is accountable for preserving the structural relationship between child and task entities; this is a data integrity obligation.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall govern the assignment of tasks to children.
payload.rationale: The enterprise is accountable for the governance rules controlling which tasks may be assigned to which children.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish tasks with associated rewards.
payload.rationale: Task definition process creates work opportunities with monetary rewards, establishing the foundational relationship between tasks and compensation.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide parents visibility into child compensation status.
payload.rationale: Oversight reporting process enables parents to view and monitor the state of compensation commitments to their children.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognize task completion as triggering reward eligibility.
payload.rationale: Task completion event establishes the condition under which a child becomes eligible to receive the associated reward.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable parents to fulfill compensation commitments to children.
payload.rationale: Enterprise goal constrains the system to support parents in meeting their compensation obligations to children.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain records of task completion.
payload.rationale: CCI-ROW1-C-How-002 describes a task completion tracking process; at Row 1 this becomes an enterprise commitment to maintain the completion records.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide parents visibility into child compensation status.
payload.rationale: CCI-ROW1-C-How-004 describes an oversight reporting process; at enterprise scope this is a commitment to enable stakeholder visibility.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain historical records enabling accountability analysis.
payload.rationale: CCI-ROW1-C-How-005 describes a historical accountability analysis process, and CCI-ROW1-C-Why-005 states the goal of providing historical accountability capability; these converge on the enterprise commitment to maintain analyzable historical records.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable trend analysis to inform pocket money system decisions.
payload.rationale: CCI-ROW1-C-Why-006 expresses a goal of enabling trend analysis for governance decisions; at Row 1 this becomes an enterprise commitment to provide that analytical capability.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish tasks assignable to children.
payload.rationale: CCI-ROW1-C-What-001 describes the 'pocket money task' entity representing work assignable to children. At Row 1, the enterprise commits to establishing the domain entity (task) as part of the child economic participation model. The entity name is 'task' per domain vocabulary; 'assignable to children' captures the structural relationship.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish rewards associated with task completion.
payload.rationale: CCI-ROW1-C-What-002 describes the 'monetary reward' entity associated with task completion. The domain noun is 'reward' (preserving source vocabulary over abstraction like 'compensation mechanism'). The enterprise commits to establishing this entity and its relationship to task completion.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain records of completed tasks for each child.
payload.rationale: CCI-ROW1-C-What-003 describes the child's accomplishment record tracking completed work. At Row 1, 'maintain records' is the appropriate enterprise-commitment verb (avoiding system-level 'track' or 'store'). The domain entity is 'completed tasks' and the subject is 'child'.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall be accountable for compensation commitments from parents to children.
payload.rationale: CCI-ROW1-C-What-006 describes the 'compensation commitment from parent to child' relationship. At Row 1, the enterprise accepts accountability for this commitment relationship (a governance/policy constraint within the parental compensation accountability domain). The domain nouns are 'compensation', 'parent', and 'child'.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to participate in the household economy.
payload.rationale: CCI-ROW1-C-Who-001 identifies the 'child user participating in pocket money economy' actor. At Row 1, the enterprise commits to enabling this participation. The domain entity is 'children' (bare noun); 'household economy' paraphrases 'pocket money economy' neutrally without coining abstract terms. 'Enable' is the appropriate enterprise-scope verb for actor participation.

payload.statement: The system shall make available tasks claimable by children.
payload.rationale: System-subject black-box affordance enabling the child claiming capability, complementary to the actor-action statement.

payload.statement: The system shall make task availability visible to children.
payload.rationale: System-subject black-box affordance providing task visibility, complementary to the actor-action statement.

payload.statement: The system shall make aggregated weekly earnings visible to children.
payload.rationale: System-subject black-box affordance providing earnings visibility, complementary to the actor-action statement.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain the assignment relationship between each completed task and the child who performed it.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility for maintaining the structural relationship between children and their completed tasks, realizing the association at the business level.

payload.statement: The business shall attribute each completed task to the specific child who performed it.
payload.rationale: Business rule governing task attribution to enforce assignment integrity.

payload.statement: Each task shall have an associated monetary value.

attributes.name: monetary_value
attributes.description: The associated monetary value for the task

payload.rationale: Structural attribute requirement defining the reward component of a task.

payload.statement: The system shall make child performance data visible to parents.
payload.rationale: System-subject black-box affordance providing parent visibility into child performance status.

payload.statement: The system shall make child earnings data visible to parents.
payload.rationale: System-subject black-box affordance providing parent visibility into child compensation status.

payload.statement: The business shall recognize task completion as establishing reward eligibility.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility for linking completion status to the earnings accumulation process, triggering reward eligibility.

payload.statement: The business shall enable payment reconciliation.
payload.rationale: Business goal constraint enabling parents to fulfill compensation commitments through reconciliation capabilities.

payload.statement: The business shall enable performance visibility.
payload.rationale: Business goal constraint providing visibility into performance to support payment reconciliation.

payload.statement: The business shall enable earnings visibility.
payload.rationale: Business goal constraint providing visibility into earnings to support payment reconciliation.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of each task completion.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility for recording task completion, preserving evidence of contribution.

payload.statement: The business shall preserve task completion history for accountability purposes.
payload.rationale: Business constraint requiring historical record retention to enable accountability analysis.

payload.statement: The business shall preserve task completion history for audit purposes.
payload.rationale: Business constraint requiring historical record retention to enable audit verification.

payload.statement: The business shall retain task completion history beyond the current reporting period.
payload.rationale: Business temporal constraint ensuring historical records span multiple periods for analysis.

payload.statement: The business shall make performance data available for trend analysis.
payload.rationale: Business capability to provide performance data supporting informed system governance decisions through trend visibility.

payload.statement: The business shall make earnings data available for trend analysis.
payload.rationale: Business capability to provide earnings data supporting informed system governance decisions through trend visibility.

payload.statement: The business shall reset task availability at weekly cycle boundaries.
payload.rationale: Business temporal constraint governing task lifecycle, realizing the governed association between children and tasks.
payload.fit_criteria: Task availability status transitions to reset state at each weekly boundary.

payload.statement: The business shall aggregate child earnings within weekly periods.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility for calculating and presenting earnings totals aligned to settlement periods, supporting financial progress understanding.

payload.statement: The system shall make task definition available to parents.
payload.rationale: System affordance enabling parents to define tasks — the capability provided at the boundary

payload.statement: The system shall make value assignment available to parents.
payload.rationale: System affordance enabling parents to assign values to tasks — the capability provided at the boundary

payload.statement: A task shall consist of a monetary value attribute.

attributes.name: monetary_value
attributes.description: A monetary value attribute that is part of the task composition

payload.rationale: Structural recursion from the task entity object — establishing tasks requires their constituent value attribute

payload.statement: A task shall have an availability status attribute.

attributes.name: availability_status
attributes.description: Status indicating the availability of the task

payload.rationale: Structural recursion — tasks assignable to children require status indicating claimability

payload.statement: The business shall enforce weekly reset of task availability.
payload.rationale: Business rule governing task lifecycle — establishing assignable tasks requires the weekly boundary constraint on their availability
payload.fit_criteria: Task availability status is reset at each weekly cycle boundary

payload.statement: A task shall be associated with a monetary value representing the reward for completion.

attributes.name: reward_value
attributes.description: A monetary value representing the reward for completion

payload.rationale: Row 2 structural expression of the task-reward association — the value attribute is the reward mechanism

payload.statement: The system shall make available tasks claimable by children.
payload.rationale: System affordance enabling child participation — the claimability mechanism provided at the boundary

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of each completed task attributed to the child who performed it.
payload.rationale: Row 2 business responsibility for completion records with attribution — core to maintaining task history per child

payload.statement: The business shall retain task completion history beyond the current reporting period.
payload.rationale: Business governance rule on record retention — maintaining completed task records requires persistence beyond immediate use

payload.statement: The business shall determine aggregated weekly earnings for each child based on completed task values.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility to settle compensation obligations — determining what is owed per the weekly settlement period

payload.statement: The system shall make aggregated weekly earnings visible to children.
payload.rationale: System affordance for earnings transparency — accountability mechanism provided at the boundary

payload.statement: The system shall make child performance data visible to parents.
payload.rationale: System affordance enabling parent oversight of child performance — one component of the accountability reporting capability

payload.statement: The system shall make child earnings data visible to parents.
payload.rationale: System affordance enabling parent oversight of child earnings — one component of the accountability reporting capability

payload.statement: The system shall make task availability visible to children.
payload.rationale: System affordance providing visibility of available tasks at the boundary to support child participation

payload.statement: The system shall make task completion status visible to children.
payload.rationale: System affordance providing visibility of task completion status at the boundary to support child participation

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a weekly period for task availability.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility to govern the temporal boundary within which tasks are available

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a weekly period for earnings accumulation.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility to govern the temporal boundary within which earnings accrue

payload.statement: The business shall enforce task availability reset at weekly cycle boundaries.
payload.rationale: Governance rule constraining task lifecycle transitions to occur at defined temporal boundaries
payload.fit_criteria: Task availability status transitions occur precisely at the weekly boundary trigger with no carry-over of prior week state

payload.statement: The system shall make available tasks claimable by entitled children.
payload.rationale: System-subject affordance enabling the actor claiming capability — complementary pair with actor statement, not a duplicate

payload.statement: The business shall attribute each completed task to the specific child who performed it.
payload.rationale: How-column attribution rule expressed as business responsibility behind the boundary

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of task completion history for each child.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility to preserve completion evidence for accountability and audit purposes across reporting periods

payload.statement: The business shall accumulate earnings for each child within the active settlement period based on completed task contributions.
payload.rationale: CCI-ROW2-C-How-003 describes the pocket money earning process whereby children accumulate funds through task completion; CCI-ROW2-C-When-001 establishes the weekly period scope. This is a core business responsibility for settlement.

payload.statement: The system shall make aggregated weekly earnings visible to the entitled child.
payload.rationale: CCI-ROW2-C-How-005 expresses the system affordance that enables the child to view earnings — the complementary system-subject capability to the actor action in REQ-D006-004. These are a boundary pair, not duplicates.

payload.statement: The business shall make the aggregated earnings report available at the end of each settlement period.
payload.rationale: CCI-ROW2-C-When-002 defines a period end event triggering availability of the aggregated earnings report — a business timing responsibility.

payload.statement: The system shall make child performance visible to parents.
payload.rationale: CCI-ROW2-C-Why-001 expresses a goal to enable payment reconciliation through visibility. This statement addresses the performance visibility component — a black-box system affordance.

payload.statement: The system shall make child earnings visible to parents.
payload.rationale: CCI-ROW2-C-Why-001 expresses a goal to enable payment reconciliation through visibility. This statement addresses the earnings visibility component — a black-box system affordance.

payload.statement: The system shall make child performance data visible to the entitled parent.
payload.rationale: System-subject affordance providing the capability that enables parent viewing of performance data; black-box capability (what the system provides, not how)

payload.statement: The system shall make child earnings data visible to the entitled parent.
payload.rationale: System-subject affordance providing the capability that enables parent payment reconciliation; black-box capability (what the system provides, not how)

payload.statement: The system shall make task completion status visible to children.
payload.rationale: System-subject statement: black-box affordance providing information visibility for task completion status.

payload.statement: The business shall recognise a parent as a business entity with authority over the pocket money system.
payload.rationale: CCI-ROW2-C-What-002 is a What-column Entity CCI asserting that Parent is a business entity carrying the structural attribute of authority over the pocket money system. This is a structural assertion about what the business entity 'Parent' is and what attribute it possesses within the business domain.

payload.statement: A child can claim an available task.
payload.rationale: Elaborates child task completion capability at Row 2 as the actor-action boundary crossing: child claiming tasks. Actor subject per boundary test.

payload.statement: The system shall make available tasks claimable by children.
payload.rationale: Complementary system-affordance enabling child task claiming. System subject naming the black-box capability provided at boundary.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of each task completion attributed to the child who performed it.
payload.rationale: Behind-boundary business responsibility for recording task completion with attribution. Business subject per boundary test.

payload.statement: The business shall enforce the attribution of completed tasks to the specific child who performed them.
payload.rationale: Business governance rule constraining task attribution. Why-column rule expressed as Constraint.

payload.statement: A child can view their aggregated weekly earnings.
payload.rationale: Actor-action boundary crossing for child viewing earnings accomplishment. Actor subject per boundary test.

payload.statement: A child can view task availability and completion status.
payload.rationale: Actor-action for child viewing accomplishment status information. Actor subject per boundary test.

payload.statement: The system shall make aggregated weekly earnings visible to the entitled child.
payload.rationale: System-affordance providing earnings visibility capability. System subject naming black-box capability.

payload.statement: A parent can view child performance and earnings data.
payload.rationale: Actor-action boundary crossing for parent viewing compensation status. Actor subject per boundary test.

payload.statement: The system shall make child performance and earnings data visible to parents.
payload.rationale: System-affordance enabling parent visibility into compensation status. System subject naming black-box reporting capability.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain aggregated weekly earnings totals for each child.
payload.rationale: Behind-boundary business responsibility for maintaining the compensation data parents view. Business subject per boundary test.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Requirement elements present in the ledger.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL DataDictionaryEntry elements present in the ledger.

payload.title: High Level Description
payload.description: Section: High Level Description

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Segment elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Expresses the core purpose of enabling children to claim and track pocket money tasks through completion.
Describes the parental motivation to define tasks with associated monetary rewards for completion.
Articulates the purpose of providing children visibility into available opportunities and their own accomplishments.
Captures the objective of enabling children to understand their weekly earnings achievement.
Defines the parental need for oversight reporting to fulfill the compensation commitment to each child.
Indicates a stakeholder requirement for historical accountability and trend analysis capability.
Section header indicating the conceptual overview of the system's purpose and capabilities.
Core conceptual capability defining the business process of children claiming tasks and earning pocket money throughout the week.
Conceptual capability describing parent authority to define tasks and their associated monetary values.
Business rule establishing the conceptual relationship between completed tasks and the child entity who performed them.
Conceptual information visibility requirement defining what data children can view regarding task availability and completion status.
Conceptual reporting capability enabling children to view aggregated weekly earnings at period end.
Conceptual reporting capability for parents to view child performance and earnings data to support payment reconciliation.
Business rule defining the temporal cycle whereby task availability resets at weekly boundaries.
Data retention requirement mandating that task completion history be preserved beyond the current reporting period.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Signal elements present in the ledger.
This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Stakeholder elements present in the ledger.
